claims.claim: Curcumin reduces chronic low-grade inflammation by inhibiting the NF-kB signaling pathway
claims.mechanism: Curcumin blocks IkB kinase, preventing NF-kB translocation to the nucleus and subsequent transcription of pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6, IL-1beta)
claims.study_ref: Aggarwal BB, Harikumar KB. Int J Biochem Cell Biol. 2009;41(1):40-59

claims.claim: Regular turmeric consumption is associated with lower rates of Alzheimer's disease in populations with high dietary intake
claims.mechanism: Curcumin crosses the blood-brain barrier and reduces amyloid-beta plaque accumulation through multiple pathways including anti-oxidant activity and metal chelation
claims.study_ref: Mishra S, Palanivelu K. Ann Indian Acad Neurol. 2008;11(1):13-19

claims.claim: Curcumin bioavailability increases up to 2000% when consumed with piperine from black pepper
claims.mechanism: Piperine inhibits hepatic and intestinal glucuronidation of curcumin, preventing rapid metabolic clearance
claims.study_ref: Shoba G et al. Planta Med. 1998;64(4):353-356

consumption.amount: 1-2 teaspoons ground turmeric daily, or a thumb-sized piece of fresh root
consumption.preparation: Cook with fat (olive oil or coconut oil) and black pepper to maximize absorption. Heat activates certain curcuminoids.
consumption.reasoning: Curcumin is fat-soluble and poorly absorbed on its own. Combining with fat and piperine addresses both bioavailability barriers.

relationships.description: Piperine in black pepper increases curcumin bioavailability by 2000% by inhibiting glucuronidation
Fat-soluble curcuminoids require dietary fat for absorption; olive oil also provides its own anti-inflammatory oleocanthal
Both are rhizomes with complementary anti-inflammatory mechanisms; ginger targets COX-2 while turmeric targets NF-kB
